English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Hospital for Animals records
Cornell University Hospital for Animals
24-6-3313
1.3 cubic feet. 1.3 cubic feet.
1979-2005. Minutes, handbooks, videocassettes. Minutes of the Hospital Board and Section Chief meetings, as well as the annual "Student Protocol," a handbook of policies and procedures for fourth year DVM students, and one videocassette from the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Equine Performance Testing Unit. Also brochures from the hospital. Collection material in English 4912375
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Laura Finkel
Date completed:
November 2003
EAD encoding:
Laura Finkel, November 12, 2003
Date modified:
Jude Corina, November 2013
Date modified:
Eileen Keating, March 2016
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The Cornell University Hospital for Animals (CUHA) is a unit of the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine. It comprises the Companion Animal Hospital, the Equine Hospital and the Farm Animal Hospital. Specialized units within the hospital include Wildlife and Exotic Animal Medicine, Ambulatory and Production Medicine, and Behavioral Medicine. It is currently housed in the Veterinary Education Center, which was opened in 1996. It was formerly named the Cornell University Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital, but was renamed in 2000.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
This collection includes minutes of the Hospital Board and Section Chief meetings, as well as the annual "Student Protocol," a handbook of policies and procedures for fourth year DVM students, and one videocassette from the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Equine Performance Testing Unit. Also brochures from the hospital.
Also photographs and slides.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Hospital Board Meeting Minutes
Series II. Section Chief Meeting Minutes
Series III. Student Protocol *The Student Protocol handbooks have been alternately called the "Student Procedure and Policy Manual" and "Student Responsibilities and General Information."
Series IV. Miscellaneous
Series V. Photographs and Slides Box 2
Cite As:
Cornell University Hospital for Animals Records, #24-06-3313. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Veterinary hospitals -- New York (State) Veterinary medicine -- Study and teaching. Cornell University Hospital for Animals
